title: Fractions on the number line
description: Plot and spot fractions on the number line.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point is at $\dfrac{4}{​6}$ on the number line?**
 
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The denominator in $\dfrac{4}{6}$ is $6$. 

So, there should be $6$ equal parts in each whole.



[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator in $\dfrac{4}{6}$ is $4$. 

So, we need to move $\dfrac16$ on the number line $\blueD4$ times to reach the point.



[[☃ image 1]]

Which number line shows a point located at $\dfrac{4}{6}$?
Number line $\greenD{A}$ shows a point located is at $\dfrac46.$



[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: $\greenD{A}$ ![A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, five unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled 1, and three unlabeled tick marks. There is a green dot at the fourth tick mark after 0. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2fe38fd36dbfa6fa2ab9b3557dc4566f38e7e01d)

$\maroonD{B}$ ![A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, five unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled 1, and three unlabeled tick marks. There is a pink dot at the third tick mark after 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0d53d94851de565da0145c3ccbfc893d89943295)

$\blueD{C}$  ![A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, eleven unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled 1, and six unlabeled tick marks. There is a blue dot at the fourth tick mark after 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d4bf825d18d3152e9b15467bfe2a8a23d6d6af6)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point is at $\dfrac{2}{​3}$ on the number line?**

hints:

[[☃ image 1]]

So, we need to move $\dfrac13$ on the number line $\blueD2$ times to reach the point.



[[☃ image 1]]

Which number line shows a point located at $\dfrac{2}{3}$?
Number line $\maroonD{B}$ shows a point located is at $\dfrac23.$



[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: $\green{A}$ ![A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, three unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at 1. There is a green dot at the second tick mark after 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/74a55e33b10b906b9fcb505628d085b5c36fe79c)

$\maroonD{B}$ ![A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, two unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at 1. There is a pink dot at the second tick mark after 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/81761bda89624ed297f258dd35d181192da4e550)

$\blueD{C}$ ![A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, five unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at 1. There is a blue dot at the second tick mark after 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/de59aceba84dd854e7ec602dcf9746fe24bf4874)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $\dfrac{1}{2}$ on the number line.**  
*First, change the number of divisions to create tick marks on the number line.*

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints:
The denominator in $\dfrac{1}{2}$ is $2$. 

So, there should be $2$ equal parts in each whole.



[[☃ image 1]]

How many times do we need to move $\dfrac{1}{2}$ on the number line to get to $\dfrac{1}{2}$?
We need to move  $\dfrac12$ on the number line $1$ time to reach $\blueD{\dfrac12}$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

The correct number line should look like this:  

Number of divisions: $2$  


[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fraction is located at Point $\blueD{A}$ on the number line?**

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{A} =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Each whole has $4$ equal parts, so each part has a length of $\dfrac{1}{4}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

We need to move  $\dfrac14$ on the number line $\blueD3$ times to reach the point.  



[[☃ image 1]]

Point $A$ is located at $\dfrac{3}{4}$.

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, three unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled 1, three unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at 2. Point A appears at the third tick mark after 0.
